name: Portabello Orzo
recipeIngredient: Salt and crushed red pepper to taste
recipeInstructions:
1. In a bowl, reconstitute the sundried tomatoes with about a cup of water (rinse them first). Set aside.
2. Wash the portabella mushrooms and cut off the stems. Place the caps in a dish and sprinkle with the three herbs. Pour the white wine over the mushrooms to marinate.
3. Coarsely chop the mushroom stems and mince the garlic. In a nonstick pan, sauté the garlic and mushroom stems in tomato water or wine.
4. Drain the tomatoes, reserving the liquid, and coarsely chop them (they don't need to be fully softened). Sauté with the remaining ingredients.
5. Boil water for cooking the orzo. Add the orzo to the boiling water and cook until done.
6. Remove the mushroom caps to plates. Pour the leftover marinating wine into the pan, add capers, and cook until reduced.
7. Drain the orzo and toss with the mixture. Serve with the mushrooms.
